Recent Update History

31 May 2026Data updateProvinces

Provincial unemployment and comparative indicators refreshed using the latest QLFS-aligned release.

Official source
31 May 2026Data updateUnemployment

Metadata verification run completed for unemployment indicators and source attribution.

Official source
31 May 2026Data updateYouth Unemployment

Youth unemployment and NEET series validated against the latest QLFS publication.

Official source
31 May 2026Data updateLabour Force Participation

Labour force participation indicators verified and aligned with QLFS metadata.

Official source
31 May 2026Data updateInflation & CPI

Inflation dataset metadata validated for monthly CPI release tracking.

Official source
31 May 2026Data updateGDP & Economic Growth

GDP quarterly indicators and coverage range validated for registry consistency.

Official source
31 May 2026MethodologyInterest Rates

Registry and lookup alignment updated for SARB repo/prime dataset identifier consistency.

Official source
1 May 2025Data updateCrime Statistics

Annual SAPS crime series refreshed with the latest published reporting cycle.

Official source
15 January 2025Data updateEducation

NSC exam results and education indicators updated from latest DBE release.

Official source
30 July 2024Data updatePopulation

Population indicators aligned with latest mid-year population estimates publication.

Official source
10 October 2023Data updateHousing & Services

Housing and services indicators updated from Census 2022 and household survey sources.

Official source
10 October 2023New datasetCensus 2022

Census 2022 baseline dataset published and marked as static until the next census cycle.

Official source

Automation notes

Status labels are derived from each dataset's metadata (`lastUpdated` + `updateFrequency`), not manually assigned. See Methodology for source-specific collection and release patterns.

Registry source of truth: 12 dataset entries with update cadence and provenance metadata.

SA Data Hub

A modern, accessible platform for exploring South African public data. Built to make government statistics understandable for everyone.

Data sourced from Statistics South Africa, SARB, SAPS and other official bodies. Not affiliated with any government department.
